mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes, providing a way to manage Kafka clusters easily. It is ideal for users looking to deploy Kafka in a Kubernetes environment, but requires familiarity with Kubernetes concepts. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Kafka management versus the flexibility and scalability offered by Kubernetes.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Strimzi.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Strimzi Kafka Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Lists all pods in the kafka namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml

```

> Deploys a Kafka cluster with persistent storage.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This command checks if the Kafka cluster is up and running.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Lists Kafka clusters in the kafka namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and any data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Kafka data. This step will upgrade the operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Backup existing Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Verifies that the upgraded operator is running.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the Strimzi Kafka Operator'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Helm repository is not added correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm repository is added and updated with 'helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/' and 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster not starting: 'Pending' status
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to insufficient resources in the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in kafka namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kafka cluster has not been created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Kafka cluster configuration using 'kubectl apply -f <kafka-config-file>'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the Kafka custom resource.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of 'KafkaUser' CRD in favor of 'KafkaUser' with new configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43, review the new configuration options for 'KafkaUser' CRD.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka', and the status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
